provenance_notes: Codex inspected public base-to-head diffs and authored claim_id, claim_type, claim_text, test_scope, and evidence_items.
cgpro provided decision guidance only; cgpro/provider output is not non-LLM evidence.
Yann has not independently reviewed each selected case after diff inspection, so these records keep human_review_required=true.

deterministic_partition_rule.rule: sort selected case_ids lexicographically; compute sha256('g6d2-holdout:' + case_id); lowest hash becomes holdout, others train

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #1695 (Typer), Rich-formatted pretty exceptions no longer truncate long source-code lines. `typer.rich_utils.get_traceback()` passes `code_width=None` and `word_wrap=True` to Rich's Traceback, so a wide terminal can show the full source line while narrower terminals wrap it instead of cutting it off.
diff_notes: PR typer#1695 changes typer/rich_utils.py so get_traceback() passes code_width=None and word_wrap=True into Rich Traceback.

New tests/test_tracebacks.py tests run tests/assets/type_error_rich.py through coverage with controlled TERMINAL_WIDTH values and assert long source lines are preserved or wrapped rather than truncated.

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #653 (sqlite-utils), upsert operations use SQLite ON CONFLICT syntax when supported, while preserving the older INSERT OR IGNORE plus UPDATE path behind `Database(..., use_old_upsert=True)`. The implementation probes `supports_on_conflict`, builds one ON CONFLICT statement for supported databases, and keeps the compatibility path for older SQLite.
diff_notes: PR sqlite-utils#653 changes sqlite_utils/db.py to add supports_on_conflict probing, Database.use_old_upsert, and ON CONFLICT SQL generation for upserts.
tests/test_upsert.py::test_upsert is parametrized over use_old_upsert False/True and asserts table.upsert(..., alter=True) updates a row in both paths.

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #757 (structlog), `ConsoleRenderer.columns` can be read and assigned after instantiation. The setter validates that exactly one default column formatter is present, stores non-default columns internally, and the getter reconstructs the default column plus configured columns.
diff_notes: PR structlog#757 changes ConsoleRenderer initialization to route custom columns through a new columns property and adds getter/setter validation in src/structlog/dev.py.
New test tests/test_dev.py::TestConsoleRenderer::test_columns_property assigns columns on an existing renderer and asserts the public property and internal formatter state align.

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #756 (structlog), `ConsoleRenderer.sort_keys` is exposed as a mutable property. The getter returns `_sort_keys`, and the setter updates `_sort_keys`, allowing callers to toggle key sorting on an existing renderer without re-instantiating it.
diff_notes: PR structlog#756 changes src/structlog/dev.py to add a ConsoleRenderer.sort_keys getter and setter around existing _sort_keys state.
New test tests/test_dev.py::TestConsoleRenderer::test_sort_keys_property toggles sort_keys False and True and checks the public property and internal state stay aligned.

deferred_diff_inspected_candidates.reason: Valid future candidate for db.table()/db.view() separation, but broader docs/API/tracer churn made it less narrow than the selected four-case tranche.
Valid future candidate for colors/force_colors properties, but deferred to avoid over-selecting ConsoleRenderer property cases in this tranche.
Implementation diff is narrow but changed_files_list has no test file; deferred until a narrow runnable upstream test scope can be established without inventing evidence.

metadata_reject_groups.reason: requires known clone-helper carve-out or older requirements pattern
